text:
Once upon a time in a quiet village nestled under big trees, lived an imaginative fox named Flopsy. Every morning Flopsy would hop through the leaves chasing imaginary moths. One day, a wise owl named Hooty hooteted.

"Hooooty?" Flopsy asked.

"I hear tales," said Hooty. "There's a moonbat that sings songs so lovely you could dance all night. And somewhere deep in the woods lives a badger who speaks every language. Listen real close."

Flopsy sat down beside Hooty.

"Hoo?"

"H'Owl-tALK?"

"Hootchie?" Flopsy laughed.

"You're ears are pretty big," said Hooty. "Like floppy hats. Listen."

Flopsy listened. Every night she could hear gentle melodies. One day she listened so hard she fell asleep. When she woke up—

"A chorus!" She exclaimed. There were dozens of little birds sitting around singing!

"One more?"

"No!"

And so Flopsy
